results.original_prompt: I want you to write me a function in JavaScript that will take an array of numbers as input and then go through each number one by one and check if that number is greater than 10 and if it is then add it to a new array and at the end return that new array with all the numbers that are greater than 10

seer_meta.optimized: Write a JavaScript function that filters an array of numbers, returning only values greater than 10.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundant descriptions of iteration mechanics ('go through each number one by one', 'at the end return') and replaced verbose logic explanation with concise requirement. Preserved exact intent.

seer_meta.optimized: Create a REST API endpoint that accepts feedback submissions, stores them in a database, and sends admin email notifications.

results.original_prompt: I would like you to help me build an authentication system for my Next.js application. The system should support multiple authentication methods including email and password login, Google OAuth, and GitHub OAuth. When a user signs up with email and password, we need to hash the password using bcrypt before storing it. For OAuth providers, we need to handle the callback URLs properly. After successful authentication, we should generate a JWT token that expires in 7 days and store it in an HTTP-only cookie. The system should also include middleware that checks for the JWT token on protected routes and redirects unauthenticated users to the login page. We also need password reset functionality where users can request a password reset link that gets sent to their email and expires after 1 hour.
results.optimized_prompt: Build Next.js auth system with: email/password (bcrypt), Google/GitHub OAuth, JWT tokens (7-day expiry in HTTP-only cookies), protected route middleware with login redirect, password reset via email (1-hour expiry).

seer_meta.optimized: Build Next.js auth system with: email/password (bcrypt), Google/GitHub OAuth, JWT tokens (7-day expiry in HTTP-only cookies), protected route middleware with login redirect, password reset via email (1-hour expiry).
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundant phrases ('help me', 'we need to', 'should'), converted narrative to structured feature list, eliminated repetition while preserving all technical requirements.

results.original_prompt: I want to create a real-time chat application using WebSockets. The application should allow users to create chat rooms, join existing rooms, and send messages in real-time. Each message should show the sender's name, timestamp, and the message content. Users should be able to see who is currently online in each room. When a user joins a room, they should see the last 50 messages from the message history. The system should handle disconnections gracefully and notify other users when someone leaves. I also want to add support for typing indicators so users can see when someone else is typing a message. Messages should be stored in a PostgreSQL database for persistence.
results.optimized_prompt: Build real-time WebSocket chat app with: room creation/joining, messages (sender name, timestamp, content), online user list, last 50 message history on join, graceful disconnection handling with notifications, typing indicators, and PostgreSQL persistence.

seer_meta.optimized: Build real-time WebSocket chat app with: room creation/joining, messages (sender name, timestamp, content), online user list, last 50 message history on join, graceful disconnection handling with notifications, typing indicators, and PostgreSQL persistence.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundancy ('should', 'users can', 'the system'), converted descriptive phrases to compact lists, eliminated filler ('I want to', 'Each message should', 'When a user'). Preserved all technical requirements.

results.original_prompt: Please help me set up a CI/CD pipeline for my Node.js project using GitHub Actions. The pipeline should run on every push to main and on pull requests. First, it should run the linting checks using ESLint. Then it should run the unit tests using Jest and generate a coverage report. If the tests pass, it should build the Docker image and tag it with the commit SHA. After building, it should push the image to AWS ECR. Finally, it should deploy the new image to our ECS cluster using a rolling update strategy. The pipeline should also send a Slack notification on failure. For pull requests, it should only run linting and tests without deploying.
results.optimized_prompt: Create GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Node.js: on push to main and PRs, run ESLint and Jest with coverage. On main only: build Docker image (tag: commit SHA), push to AWS ECR, deploy to ECS (rolling update). Send Slack notification on failure. PRs skip deployment.

seer_meta.optimized: Create GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Node.js: on push to main and PRs, run ESLint and Jest with coverage. On main only: build Docker image (tag: commit SHA), push to AWS ECR, deploy to ECS (rolling update). Send Slack notification on failure. PRs skip deployment.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundancy ('Please help me', 'The pipeline should'), combined related steps into concise clauses, eliminated filler phrases ('First', 'Then', 'After building'), and replaced verbose descriptions with technical shorthand.

results.original_prompt: My React app is rendering the component twice on every state update. I'm using useEffect with an empty dependency array but it still fires twice. The component fetches data from an API and sometimes shows stale data. I think there might be a race condition. Help me fix this.
results.optimized_prompt: React component renders twice on state updates despite useEffect with empty dependency array. API calls cause stale data; possible race condition. Fix needed.

seer_meta.optimized: React component renders twice on state updates despite useEffect with empty dependency array. API calls cause stale data; possible race condition. Fix needed.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed explanatory phrases ('I'm using', 'I think there might be'), redundant details ('sometimes shows'), and converted to direct problem statement. Preserved all technical specifics: double render, useEffect, empty dependency array, API/stale data, race condition.

seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundancy ('all', 'mixed together'), filler phrases ('proper layers', 'following clean architecture principles'), and condensed into actionable technical requirements.
